title: Chat messages render as plain text — no markdown or code highlighting
body:
## Problem
The `Bubble` component in `src/renderer/src/modules/assistant/AssistantHome.tsx:253-276` renders message content as a single `<div>` with `whitespace-pre-wrap`. No markdown parsing, no code-block syntax highlighting, no LaTeX, no tables. Analyst-grade LLM responses — which almost always include headings, bullet lists, code snippets, tables of financial data — render as an unformatted wall of text. Unusable for a finance power-user.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/assistant/AssistantHome.tsx:253-276` — Bubble returns raw string
- System prompts in `src/renderer/src/lib/systemPrompts.ts` explicitly ask for markdown structure, yet renderer ignores it

## Expected
Messages render with markdown (headings, lists, tables, links), code blocks with syntax highlighting (at minimum for JSON, Python, TS, SQL), inline code styled distinctly, and safe HTML sanitization.

## Proposed fix
1. Add `react-markdown` + `remark-gfm` + `rehype-highlight` (or Shiki) to deps
2. Replace the Bubble content node with `<ReactMarkdown>` + custom renderers for `code` (wrap with copy button), `table` (add scroll-wrapper), `a` (ensure `target=_blank rel=noreferrer`)
3. Pipe sanitization via `rehype-sanitize` to block raw HTML from injected content
4. Preserve copy-friendly formatting (tables round-trip to plain text cleanly)

## Impact
Users currently cannot read structured responses without manually mentally reflowing them; the app looks a generation behind ChatGPT / Claude.ai / Cursor.

title: No copy-to-clipboard on messages or code blocks
body:
## Problem
Neither the `Bubble` component nor any child element offers a copy action. Users must manually drag-select text, which in a scroll container with streaming updates is flaky — selection gets cleared when new tokens arrive and re-layout happens.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/assistant/AssistantHome.tsx:253-276` — no copy button or keyboard shortcut
- Finance workflows: user wants to paste a generated portfolio review into journal, or paste a code snippet into VS Code

## Expected
Hover-visible copy button on each message (icon top-right, appears on hover). Each code block has its own copy button. Keyboard shortcut `Cmd+Shift+C` copies last assistant message. Visual feedback (checkmark + tooltip) confirms copy.

## Proposed fix
1. Add a `CopyButton` component wrapping `navigator.clipboard.writeText`
2. Mount once per Bubble (full message) and once per markdown code block via custom renderer
3. Show checkmark for 1500ms on success
4. Register global shortcut via existing useEffect key handler pattern

## Impact
Core productivity gap for a desktop assistant targeting serious users.

title: Auto-scroll jumps to bottom even when user scrolled up to read
body:
## Problem
`AssistantHome.tsx:62-64` has a scroll effect that fires on every message update, forcibly scrolling to bottom during streaming. If the user scrolled up to re-read an earlier answer while the LLM is still generating, every new token yanks the viewport back down. Effectively unusable during long responses.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/assistant/AssistantHome.tsx:62-64` — unconditional `scrollToBottom`
- `AssistantHome.tsx:186` — container without scroll-position tracking

## Expected
Auto-scroll only when user is already near the bottom (≤100px). If user scrolled up, show a pill button 'New messages ↓' that jumps to bottom on click. Pause auto-scroll until user opts back in.

## Proposed fix
1. Track `isAtBottom` via scroll event (debounced)
2. Only auto-scroll when `isAtBottom` is true
3. Render a floating pill when `!isAtBottom && newMessages > 0`
4. Click clears pill, scrolls to bottom, resumes auto-scroll

## Impact
Long responses are effectively unreadable during streaming; user loses context and gets frustrated.

title: Streaming indicator is a bare '…' — indistinguishable from loading
body:
## Problem
`AssistantHome.tsx:200-202` renders three dots `...` during streaming. Same visual is used for pre-response loading, active generation, and idle moments. No animation, no color, no distinction. Users can't tell if the connection died, if the model is thinking, or if tokens are still flowing.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/assistant/AssistantHome.tsx:200-202` — static ellipsis
- `AssistantHome.tsx:271` — no cursor inside the active bubble

## Expected
Active streaming shows a pulsing caret inside the bubble (like cursor blinking). Between turns, show a 'Thinking' state with animated dots or shimmer. Show elapsed-time indicator after 3s for user reassurance ('Thinking 7s…').

## Proposed fix
1. Replace '…' with a dedicated `<StreamingCursor>` component that uses CSS animation
2. Add state machine: idle | thinking | streaming | complete | error
3. Render per-state indicator
4. After 3s without tokens, start elapsed timer

## Impact
Users hit Stop or retry when the model was merely slow — wasted tokens + worse UX.

title: Composer missing Shift+Enter, Cmd+Enter, Cmd+K, up-arrow edit
body:
## Problem
`AssistantHome.tsx:213-228` input has no keyboard shortcut handling. Enter alone sends — fine — but Shift+Enter does nothing (should insert newline), Cmd/Ctrl+Enter does nothing (should also send), Cmd+K does nothing (should start new conversation), up-arrow on empty input does nothing (should edit last user message, like ChatGPT).

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/assistant/AssistantHome.tsx:213-228` — textarea with no keydown handler beyond Enter

## Expected
Standard chat-composer shortcut matrix: Enter send, Shift+Enter newline, Cmd/Ctrl+Enter send (even when multi-line), Cmd+K new conversation, up-arrow-on-empty edit last user msg, Esc blur.

## Proposed fix
Add keydown handler: branch on `e.key`, `e.shiftKey`, `e.metaKey`, `e.ctrlKey`. Use a ref to last user message for edit flow. Guard up-arrow only firing when input is empty (otherwise cursor nav).

## Impact
Power-user workflows blocked; current behavior feels like a 2015-era chatbox.

title: Conversation sidebar lacks search, rename, export, bulk delete
body:
## Problem
The conversation sidebar (`AssistantHome.tsx:123-183`) lists conversations but offers no search, no rename, no export, no delete (beyond perhaps a single-item action), no sort options. As conversation count grows beyond ~20, finding a past thread requires scrolling and guessing from auto-generated titles.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/assistant/AssistantHome.tsx:123-183` — sidebar renders list only
- No `useConversations` operations for rename or bulk delete exposed

## Expected
Search input that fuzzy-matches titles + message content. Right-click or ⋯ menu per row: Rename, Export as Markdown, Duplicate, Delete. Bulk-select via checkbox + delete-all. Sort by recency / length / model used.

## Proposed fix
1. Add a top-of-sidebar search input, debounced fuzzy filter (use `fuse.js` or simple includes)
2. Expose `renameConversation`, `deleteConversations(ids[])`, `exportConversation` in hook + IPC
3. Render a dropdown menu per row + bulk-select mode toggle
4. Export formats: Markdown, JSON

## Impact
Sidebar becomes unusable beyond modest conversation volume — user abandons assistant for one-offs.

title: Model selector lacks context window + cost indicators
body:
## Problem
`AssistantHome.tsx:136-148` shows model name only. User can't see: context window size (8k vs 200k), input/output pricing, whether the model supports tools/vision, whether it's the latest version. This matters when picking between Haiku (cheap, small) and Opus (expensive, huge) for a portfolio review.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/assistant/AssistantHome.tsx:136-148` — label-only dropdown
- `src/renderer/src/lib/constants.ts:28-34` — MODEL constants lack metadata

## Expected
Each option shows: model name, context window (e.g., '200K ctx'), relative cost badge ($, $$, $$$), capability icons (tools, vision). Tooltip explains tradeoffs.

## Proposed fix
1. Extend model constants into `{id, label, provider, contextWindow, inputPricePerM, outputPricePerM, supportsTools, supportsVision}`
2. Render option as a two-line item with badges
3. Sort groups by provider, order within group by capability tier

## Impact
Users pick wrong model and either hit context overflow or overpay by 10×.

title: Error messages shown as raw provider text — not actionable
body:
## Problem
`AssistantHome.tsx:203-206` and `useAI.ts:92-98` render `error.message` directly. Users see `401 Unauthorized` (should be 'Invalid API key — check Settings'), `429 Too Many Requests` (should be 'Rate limit hit — retrying in X s'), or `TypeError: fetch failed` (should be 'No internet connection').

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/assistant/AssistantHome.tsx:203-206` — raw message
- `src/renderer/src/hooks/useAI.ts:92-98` — no error classification

## Expected
Categorized errors with friendly copy + action button (Open Settings / Retry / Check Network).

## Proposed fix
Add an `errorMapper(rawError): {kind, userMessage, actionLabel?, onAction?}`. Kinds: auth, rate_limit, network, context_overflow, server, unknown. Render a dedicated `<AssistantError>` bubble with action.

## Impact
Users bounce on first error because they don't know what went wrong.

title: Stop button has no feedback — cancellation feels unresponsive
body:
## Problem
`AssistantHome.tsx:230-235` has a Stop button that calls `useAI.ts:55-61` cancel. No visual feedback on click — no spinner, no disabled state, no 'Stopping…' label. If the stop takes >200ms (network, provider latency), user clicks again or assumes it's broken.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/assistant/AssistantHome.tsx:230-235` — one-shot button
- `src/renderer/src/hooks/useAI.ts:55-61` — cancel without state transition

## Expected
Click disables button, changes label to 'Stopping…' with spinner, fades bubble opacity, and on actual abort shows a 'Stopped' marker in the bubble. If stop fails within 3s, show retry.

## Proposed fix
Add `cancelling` state in useAI. Button renders based on state: 'Stop' → 'Stopping…' (disabled, spinner) → hidden. Mark truncated bubbles with a strikethrough or '⏹ stopped' footer.

## Impact
Stuttering clicks double-fire cancel and confuse users.

title: Meeting notes transcript and summary share same edit state — lossy
body:
## Problem
`MeetingNotes.tsx:142-187` renders transcript and AI-generated summary in editable fields that share state without versioning. Editing summary after regeneration overwrites prior edits; regenerating clobbers user annotations; no 'Copy summary' action; no export.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/assistant/MeetingNotes.tsx:142-187` — single state blob, no history

## Expected
Transcript and summary are separate persistable blocks. Regenerate summary prompts 'You have unsaved edits — overwrite?'. Per-block copy + export. Revision history (last 5 versions) accessible via a history button.

## Proposed fix
1. Split state into `transcript`, `summary`, `summaryRevisions[]`
2. Guard regenerate with dirty-check confirm
3. Persist revisions on each regenerate (cap at 5)
4. Add copy + export buttons per block

## Impact
Users lose curated notes when trying to iterate the summary — silent data loss.
